heroHeadline: TTP North Waziristan Battle Day 2 (71 Killed); Trump Congo Plan for Afghan Allies Sparks Outrage; Non-Escalation Holds Day 17
heroSubtitle: Pakistani security forces continued to repel a major TTP cross-border infiltration column from Afghanistan on April 26 (Day 2 of a three-day battle), with the operation ultimately killing 71 TTP militants in North Waziristan — the largest TTP incursion since the Urumqi framework took hold. The inter-state non-escalation between Pakistan and Afghanistan's Islamic Emirate reached Day 17, sustaining the longest unbroken lull since Operation Ghazab-il-Haq began. Pakistan and Iran deported over 2,200 Afghans in a single day — with 4,088 expelled in just two days — as Trump's reported plan to relocate 1,100 Afghan US war allies from Qatar to the Democratic Republic of Congo sparked intense bipartisan outrage. Urumqi Round 2 remains on track for mid-to-late May.
footerNote: Data sourced from UNAMA, SIGAR, ACLED, Brown University Costs of War Project, US DoD, Reuters, BBC, Tolo News, Dawn, Al Jazeera, HRW, OHCHR
